title: Phenol-chloroform DNA extraction protocol for biodiversity
author: Mateus Chamone MCB Burgarelli
materials: Lysis Solution 1 (100 mM Tris-HCl, 10 mM EDTA, 100 mM NaCl, 1% SDS)
Discard the supernatant and retain the pellet.

steps.title: Transfer a small piece of tissue or blood into 1
steps.instruction: Transfer a small piece of tissue or blood into 1.5 mL tubes. Muscle tissue (10–25 mg); Liver (5–10 mg). Mammalian blood (20–100 µL); Avian blood (2–10 µL). Avian eggshell membrane (25–40 mg – target blood vessels). Coagulated blood: Heat the sample at 55 °C for 10 minutes, then immediately place it on ice. Invert or gently shake to disperse the clot.

steps.title: Rigid tissues
steps.instruction: Rigid tissues: Add Milli-Q H2O and incubate in a 55 °C water bath for 10–20 min. D iscard the supernatant and proceed to the next step.

steps.title: Thoroughly mince the tissue using a micro-scissor and/or pestle
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mince the tissue using a micro-scissor and/or pestle.

steps.title: Mammalian blood
steps.instruction: Mammalian blood: Add 1 mL pre-warmed Tris NH4Cl (37 °C). Gently mix by inversion, incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature, and centrifuge at 7,000 rpm for 5 minutes. Discard the supernatant and proceed.

steps.title: Add 1 mL saline solution (0.85% NaCl). For blood
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL saline solution (0.85% NaCl). For blood: Preferably use 1 mL PBS instead of saline.

steps.title: Briefly vortex three times and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex three times and centrifuge at 7,000 rpm for 5 minutes .

steps.title: Repeat steps 3–5
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 3–5.

steps.title: Add 400 µL Lysis Solution 1...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL Lysis Solution 1 (100 mM Tris-HCl, 10 mM EDTA, 100 mM NaCl, 1% SDS), pre-warmed at 55 °C.

steps.title: Add 5–15 µL Proteinase K (20 mg/mL)
steps.instruction: Add 5–15 µL Proteinase K (20 mg/mL).

steps.title: Incubate in a water bath or...
steps.instruction: Incubate in a water bath or Thermomixer at 55 °C with moderate rotation (~550 rpm) for 2–12h, or until complete digestion. If digestion is incomplete after overnight incubation, add an additional 10 µL Proteinase K and incubate for at least 2 more hours. Rigid tissues: digestion time may be extended up to 24 h.

steps.title: Add 10 µL RNase A (10 mg/mL)
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL RNase A (10 mg/mL).

steps.title: Incubate for 3 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 3 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 120 µL Milli-Q H2O
steps.instruction: Add 120 µL Milli-Q H2O.

steps.title: In a fume hood, add 1 volume Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (25:24:1)
steps.instruction: In a fume hood, add 1 volume Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (25:24:1).

steps.title: Mix vigorously for 30 seconds
steps.instruction: Mix vigorously for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube. For tissues rich in structural proteins or incomplete lysis: repeat steps 5–8.

steps.title: Add 1 volume Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (24:1)
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (24:1).

steps.title: Mix vigorously for 30 seconds
steps.instruction: Mix vigorously for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer 400 µL of the supernatant to a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer 400 µL of the supernatant to a new tube. If the aqueous phase is turbid or yellowish, repeat steps 9–12.

steps.title: Outside the fume hood, add 100...
steps.instruction: Outside the fume hood, add 100 µL Ammonium Acetate 10 M (final concentration 2 M).

steps.title: Add 1 mL ice-cold Absolute Ethanol,...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL ice-cold Absolute Ethanol, then mix well by inversion (20 times).

steps.title: Store the sample at -80 °C...
steps.instruction: Store the sample at -80 °C for at least 1 h, or overnight at -20 °C, to stimulate DNA precipitation. DNA can be indefinitely stored in ethanolic solutions at 0 °C or -20 °C. Invert the tube a few times before proceeding.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 20 minutes at 0 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 20 minutes at 0 °C.

steps.title: Carefully discard the supernatant with a...
steps.instruction: Carefully discard the supernatant with a pipette without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Add 1 mL 70% Ethanol and...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL 70% Ethanol and gently invert the tube 3 times.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 5 minutes at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 5 minutes at 4 °C. Recommended: Repeat steps 18–20.

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant with a...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant with a pipette without disturbing the pellet, and let it air dry. If necessary, use pipettes with progressively smaller volumes until all ethanol is removed. Do not let the pellet dry completely – it should remain moist before elution.

steps.title: Dissolve the pellet in an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Dissolve the pellet in an appropriate volume of Low TE or ultrapure water (20–100 µL). Preheating Low TE aliquots at 55 °C is recommended for better elution.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 1 minute.

steps.title: Incubate in a Water Bath or...
steps.instruction: Incubate in a Water Bath or Thermomixer with minimal rotation at 55 °C for 1–2 h. To remove ethanol traces, keep tubes open for 10 min at 55 °C. Alternatively, incubate overnight at room temperature after a brief pre-heating at 55 °C.

steps.title: Store the samples in the refrigerator or freezer
steps.instruction: Store the samples in the refrigerator or freezer.

steps.title: Add 10 µL RNase A (10 mg/mL)
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL RNase A (10 mg/mL).

steps.title: Incubate for 3 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 3 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 300 µL Milli-Q H2O
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL Milli-Q H2O.

steps.title: In a fume hood, add 1 volume Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (25:24:1)
steps.instruction: In a fume hood, add 1 volume Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (25:24:1).

steps.title: Mix vigorously for 30 seconds
steps.instruction: Mix vigorously for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube. For tissues rich in structural proteins or incomplete lysis: repeat steps 5–8.

steps.title: Add 1 volume Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (24:1)
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (24:1).

steps.title: Mix vigorously for 30 seconds
steps.instruction: Mix vigorously for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube. If the aqueous phase is turbid or yellowish, repeat steps 9–12.

steps.title: Outside the fume hood, add Ammonium...
steps.instruction: Outside the fume hood, add Ammonium Acetate 10 M to a final concentration of 2–2.5 M. Tip: For a final concentration of 2 M, add 1/4 of the recovered volume of 10 M A.A.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.7–1 volume of room-temperature Isopropanol, then mix well by inversion (20 times).

steps.title: Incubate for 3 minutes at room temperature, inverting occasionally
steps.instruction: Incubate for 3 minutes at room temperature, inverting occasionally.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 25 minutes at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 25 minutes at 4 °C.

steps.title: Carefully discard the supernatant with a...
steps.instruction: Carefully discard the supernatant with a pipette without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Add 1 mL 70% Ethanol at...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL 70% Ethanol at room temperature and gently invert the tube 3 times.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 5–10 minutes at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 5–10 minutes at 4 °C.

steps.title: Repeat steps 18–20
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 18–20.

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant with a...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant with a pipette without disturbing the pellet, and let it air dry. If necessary, use pipettes with progressively smaller volumes until all ethanol is removed. Do not let the pellet dry completely – it should remain moist before elution.

steps.title: Dissolve the pellet in an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Dissolve the pellet in an appropriate volume of Low TE or ultrapure water (20–100 µL). Preheating Low TE aliquots at 55 °C is recommended for better elution.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 1 minute.

steps.title: Incubate in a Water Bath or...
steps.instruction: Incubate in a Water Bath or Thermomixer with minimal rotation at 55 °C for 1–2 h. To remove ethanol traces, keep tubes open for 10 min at 55 °C. Alternatively, incubate overnight at room temperature after a brief pre-heating at 55 °C.

steps.title: Store the samples in the refrigerator or freezer
steps.instruction: Store the samples in the refrigerator or freezer.
